data_name: Digital Multiverse / heterogeneous digital twin artefacts
method_key: Digital Multiverse paradigm; consistency rules across models and data
collection_method: Conceptual framework and examples; no explicit public dataset released in the paper

uncertainty.U2_measurement: The paper is a conceptual integration framework rather than a benchmark dataset release. Uncertainty arises from semantic mismatches across heterogeneous digital twin artefacts (for example CAD models, control models, and data-driven models), and the paper does not quantify how consistency rules perform across all industrial scenarios.

provenance: Conceptual examples in article

data_type: Vessel trajectory table with vessel ID
collection_method: User-supplied CSV input to the web service

uncertainty.U2_measurement: User-provided vessel trajectories may contain missing timestamps, GPS/positioning errors, irregular sampling intervals, and vessel-speed inaccuracies. These issues can affect the identification of fishing behaviour and any downstream biodiversity-risk inference.

provenance: Input specification described in paper

collection_method: Public BOEM/NOAA downloadable CSV (types 30 and 1001)

uncertainty.U2_measurement: AIS/vessel tracking records may include coverage gaps, duplicated pings, positioning errors, and incomplete vessel metadata. Coastal reception quality and filtering choices can introduce uncertainty in the benchmark fishing-pressure estimates.

spatial_coverage: Northwest Atlantic benchmark area; broader source spans Alaska to Gulf of Mexico
provenance: Public source used in benchmark case study

collection_method: Satellite, AIS-terrestrial, and VIIRS data; fishing activity pre-classified by machine-learning model

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Fishing-activity estimates are inferred from multiple remote-sensing and tracking streams (satellite AIS, terrestrial AIS, and VIIRS), so misclassification and coverage gaps may occur. Spatial aggregation at grid level can smooth local fishing patterns and introduce uncertainty in change-detection results.

spatial_coverage: Global source; Northwest Atlantic and Italy cases in paper
temporal_coverage: Embedded 2012–2016 dataset; March–May 2015 benchmark; March–April 2020 derived CSV
format: Aggregated grids/raster products (0.01° and 0.1°); derived CSV in paper
license: Free-to-use for scientific purposes (as stated in paper)
provenance: Public source used in benchmark and temporal case studies

data_type: Species occurrence records and taxonomic information
collection_method: Geospatial query per fishing cell via the robis R package

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Biodiversity occurrence records are affected by uneven sampling effort, taxonomic inconsistencies, georeferencing errors, and temporal sparsity. Query-window choices around the fishing period can change which species are linked to each fishing cell.

temporal_coverage: Occurrence Time Range years before and after vessel dataset (default 5 years; experiments used 8)
provenance: FAIR enrichment source named in methodology

collection_method: Threat validation against IUCN Red List through the biodiversity enrichment step

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Threat-status matching depends on correct species-name harmonisation with the Red List. Assessment cycles, synonym issues, and lag between species observations and Red List updates may introduce uncertainty in vulnerability attribution.

temporal_coverage: Current to query time / version not specified in paper
provenance: FAIR enrichment source named in methodology

collection_method: CSV table updated to 2020 and hosted on D4Science platform storage for computation

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Taxonomic filtering depends on the completeness and currency of the ASFIS species list. Name-matching ambiguities, synonym handling, and update lag can affect whether biodiversity records are classified as fisheries-relevant species.

provenance: D4Science-hosted copy of FAO ASFIS

data_name: Global Record of Stocks and Fisheries (GRSF)

collection_method: Direct query to the SPARQL endpoint of the GRSF semantic knowledge base

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Stock linkage through the semantic knowledge base may be incomplete for some taxa or regions. Ontology alignment choices and identifier mismatches can create uncertainty when distinguishing stock-associated species from non-stock species.

provenance: i-Marine / D4Science semantic knowledge base

collection_method: Integrated as FAIR environmental data through the D4Science e-infrastructure

uncertainty.U2_measurement: Environmental context layers can differ in spatial resolution, temporal granularity, and model assumptions. Interpolation, regridding, and dataset-version differences may introduce uncertainty when linking ocean conditions to fishing cells.

provenance: FAIR enrichment source named in methodology
